title: When to Use a GAN
concept: Choose GANs when generation speed is critical (single forward pass) and you can tolerate more difficult, less stable training.

exercises.prompt: What is one of the main challenges you face when deciding to use a GAN?

options.text: Training can be unstable and difficult.
They are extremely slow to generate images.

exercises.explain: GANs consist of a generator and a discriminator competing against each other. This setup is powerful but notoriously tricky to train.

cards.title: Need for Speed
cards.body: Imagine you are building a video game that needs to generate realistic landscapes on the fly as players run. You don't have seconds to wait—you need images instantly! How do we pick the right AI tool for super-speed generation?

cards.title: When to Choose a GAN
cards.body: A Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) is a machine learning setup where two networks compete. We choose GANs when we need fast, real-time generation (it only takes one quick step, or 'forward pass') and we are prepared to handle a tricky, unstable training process to get there.

cards.title: The Painter and the Critic
cards.body: Think of a GAN like a master painter working with a harsh art critic. Instead of painstakingly planning every detail over hours (like other AI models), the painter whips up a complete canvas in one quick go. The critic instantly points out what's wrong, forcing the painter to get fast and smart. Once trained, the painter can churn out beautiful art in a single flash!

cards.title: Real-Time Art Filters
cards.body: Imagine building a photo-filter app where users instantly transform their selfies into anime art. Because users expect the photo to change the moment they tap the screen, you choose a GAN. You spend weeks carefully tuning the unstable training, but the end result is a super-fast app.

cards.title: Myth: GANs Are Always Best
cards.body: Many believe GANs are the best choice for every project because they make great images. In reality, training them is a balancing act that easily breaks (called instability). If you don't need instant generation speed, other models might save you a lot of headache!

cards.title: The GAN Trade-Off
cards.body: Compare the trade-offs of choosing a GAN versus other generative models.

exercises.prompt: Why would a developer choose a GAN over other image generators when speed is the top priority?

options.text: They generate images in a single, fast forward pass.
They are the easiest AI models to train.
They never make mistakes during training.
They run slowly but use less computer memory.

exercises.explain: GANs generate images in a single forward pass, which makes them much faster than models that generate images step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GANs are chosen because they are the easiest and most stable AI models to train.
exercises.explain: Training a GAN is known for being unstable and difficult, even though the final model generates images very quickly.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the main benefit of using a GAN for generation.
exercises.sentence: We choose GANs because they are super [blank] at generating new images.
exercises.explain: Once trained, a GAN only needs a single forward pass to create an image, making it incredibly fast.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly describes when to use a GAN?

options.text: A: Choose a GAN if you need instant generation speed and can handle tough training.
B: Choose a GAN because it is the easiest and most stable model to train.
C: Avoid GANs if you want fast speed, because they generate images step-by-step.

exercises.explain: Option A is correct because GANs generate images instantly in one pass. Option B is incorrect because they are notoriously difficult to train. Option C is incorrect because they are actually faster at generation than most rivals.

exercises.prompt: Your team needs an AI model that can generate high-quality game textures in real-time (instant generation). However, your team has extra time to handle a tricky and unstable training phase. Why is a GAN the perfect fit?

options.text: They are very fast at generating new samples, but training them can be unstable.
They are very easy to train, but extremely slow when generating new samples.
They require no training at all and generate images instantly.
They are slow at generation and also impossible to train.

exercises.explain: GANs generate images in a single forward pass, making them incredibly fast during generation. However, their training process is notorious for being unstable and difficult to balance.
